results.title: New probate rules from July mean UK families face £526 charge
results.description: Labour Party government has confirmed an increase to the probate application fee to £526.

results.title: PrintCon 2026 and Print Philippines unifies the Philippine print industry toward stronger future
results.description: PrintCon 2026 and Print Philippines concluded its three‐day unified tradeshow at the SMX Convention Center, marking a historic collaboration between the Philippine Printing Technical Foundation (PPTF) and the Printing Industries Association of the Philippines (PIAP). The event brought together exhibitors, innovators, educators, government agencies, and creative communities under one strengthened national platform. The tradeshow opened with a ribbon‐cutting led by PPTF President Dante E. Llarena and PIAP President Albert See, joined by leaders from major print and packaging organizations. A vibrant Lion and Dragon Dance welcomed guests into the exhibition halls, symbolizing renewed unity across the sector. Government Leaders Affirm Strong Support A key highlight of the event was the message delivered by DTI Undersecretary Blesila A. “Bless” Lantayona, who encouraged the industry to “continue building an industry that is innovative yet responsible, competitive yet inclusive, and profitable yet sustainable.” Her remarks reinforced the government’s commitment to supporting the growth and modernization of the Philippine print and packaging sectors. Additional support came from ASEAN‐BAC Executive Adviser Malyn Molina, who emphasized the role of printing and packaging in the creative economy, and leaders from the Philippine Trade Training Center (PTTC), who reaffirmed their mission to equip MSMEs and professionals with future‐ready skills. Strengthening Collaboration and Industry Trust Keynote speaker Josiah Go, Chairman of Mansmith & Fielders, underscored the importance of “trust infrastructure” in building a unified industry, noting that no single company can elevate Philippine printing alone — only a cohesive, collaborative sector can. The unified PrintCon–PhilPrint format also maximized exhibitor resources by eliminating duplication and strengthening the industry’s shared voice. Education, Creativity, and Global Opportunities Multi‐sectoral partners such as DTI, DOST, SB Corp, NBDB, and TESDA reinforced the event’s educational mission. Creative leaders highlighted growing global opportunities for Filipino comics and printed books, signaling strong international interest in Philippine content. Message from the Conference Chair Ms. Leah Marie Ayeng, PPTF Communications & Research Chair and PrintCon 2026 Conference Chair, shared: “PrintCon 2026 proves that unity and collaboration can move the industry forward. This is only the beginning — our goal is to build on this momentum and elevate Philippine print on the global stage.” About PPTF The Philippine Printing Technical Foundation (PPTF) is the country’s leading institution for print education, technical training, and industry capability building.

results.title: Glasgow gym and snooker club faces demolition under plans for 314 student flats
results.description: Plans to demolish Glasgow's Q Club snooker hall and PureGym for 300 student flats near Charing Cross have sparked objections from angry locals.
